title: How to Clean Your Home Before Selling in Melbourne
excerpt: First impressions sell houses. A deep pre-sale clean is one of the cheapest ways to add perceived value — here's the exact process Melbourne agents recommend.
htmlContent:
Melbourne's property market is competitive. Buyers attend multiple inspections every weekend, and properties that look and smell clean consistently receive stronger offers and faster sales. A thorough pre-sale clean is one of the cheapest investments you can make before listing.

Why Cleaning Before Selling Matters More Than You Think

Real estate agents across Melbourne consistently say that a dirty or poorly maintained property drops perceived value by 5–10% in buyers' minds — regardless of what the property is actually worth. Conversely, a spotless home signals maintenance, care, and move-in readiness.

You don't need to renovate. You need to clean.

The Pre-Sale Cleaning Checklist

Kitchen — Buyers' Top Priority

  • Degrease and clean oven inside and out (including racks, door glass, and drawer)
  • Clean rangehood filters — greasy filters are noticed immediately
  • Descale and polish sink and tapware
  • Wipe down all cabinets inside and out, paying attention to handles and edges
  • Clean behind and under the fridge if accessible
  • Wipe inside the fridge if it's staying with the property
  • Clean dishwasher filter and run a cleaning cycle
  • Polish splashback until it reflects light

Bathrooms — Second Most Scrutinised

  • Descale and polish all tapware and shower heads
  • Re-caulk or scrub silicone in shower recesses if staining is visible
  • Clean and de-mould grout lines throughout
  • Clean and polish mirrors (streak-free)
  • Remove soap scum from screens and baths
  • Deep clean toilets including under the rim and behind the base

Floors — Sets the Whole Tone

  • Professionally steam clean all carpets — even lightly used carpets show foot traffic under inspection lighting
  • Strip and re-seal timber floors if dull or scuffed
  • Clean tile grout lines (dirty grout makes clean tiles look dirty)
  • Remove scuff marks from skirting boards

Walls, Ceilings and Windows

  • Spot clean walls for marks and scuffs — magic erasers work well on painted walls
  • Clean light switches and power points (these accumulate hand grime)
  • Wipe door frames, doors, and handles throughout
  • Clean windows inside and out — natural light is one of your biggest assets
  • Clean fly screens (dirty screens diffuse light and look neglected)
  • Dust ceiling fans, light fittings, and smoke detectors

Outdoors and Entry

  • Pressure wash driveways and paths if stained
  • Clean the front door, letterbox, and porch area — this is the first thing a buyer photographs
  • Clear cobwebs from eaves and corners
  • Clean garage floor and shelving

Professional Steam Cleaning vs DIY

For carpets specifically, professional steam cleaning is worth it every time. Hire equipment gives patchy results and can leave carpets damp for days, causing odour. A professional team completes the job in 2–3 hours with hot-water extraction, leaving carpets dry within 4–6 hours and free of the odours that personal equipment can miss.

The 48-Hour Rule Before Inspections

Even a professionally cleaned home shows signs of life within 48 hours of being lived in. Before each open for inspection:

  • Air out the home for at least 2 hours (neutral smell is better than any fragrance)
  • Wipe kitchen surfaces
  • Quick vacuum of traffic areas
  • Clean bathroom mirrors and vanity tops
  • Take out rubbish
  • Remove personal toiletries, clutter, and pet accessories

What Melbourne Real Estate Agents Notice First

We spoke to several Melbourne agents, and the consistent feedback was: kitchen odours, bathroom mould, carpet stains, and dirty windows are the four things that cause the most negative buyer reactions during inspections. These are also the four areas where professional cleaning delivers the clearest ROI.
